channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Colombians flee to Venezuela as clashes between rebel groups escalate in coca-rich border region
item.description: TRES BOCAS, Colombia (AP) — The Colombian border village of Tres Bocas has become a ghost town as residents flee to neighboring Venezuela to escape a new wave of violence in Colombia’s Catatumbo region that has left at least 80 people dead and displaced thousands.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDO VERGARA and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's top general resigns over Oct. 7 failures, adding to pressure on Netanyahu
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's top general resigned Tuesday, taking responsibility for security failures tied to Hamas' surprise attack that triggered the war in Gaza and adding to pressure on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who has delayed any public inquiry that could potentially implicate his leade
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Stefanik vows to keep the U.S. engaged at U.N. to counter China
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) assured skeptical Senate Democrats during her confirmation hearing as President Trump's pick for U.N. ambassador that she is committed to engaging in the United Nations, saying it’s a priority venue to challenge China’s push for dominance on the global stage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: The rescue of hundreds of miners in a deep South African gold mine began with a camera and a note
item.description: Late last year, a specialized camera was lowered into an almost 1.6-mile-deep mineshaft in South Africa where hundreds of miners were reported to be trapped and starving.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia's president will declare an emergency over deadly ELN attacks in the northeast
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro said Monday that he will declare a state of emergency over the guerrilla attacks in the northeast that have killed dozens of people and forced thousands to flee their homes.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia sounds off on Trump’s threat to retake the Panama Canal
item.description: Russia’s foreign ministry has called on Trump to reaffirm the current international agreement surrounding the Panama Canal and to leave it in control of the nation of Panama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio's first order of business: Meeting Quad's foreign secretaries
item.description: Newly sworn-in Secretary of State Marco Rubio is hitting the ground running Tuesday with meetings with his counterparts in the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue, known as the Quad, which includes India, Australia and Japan, a bloc formed to counter Chinese clout in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Mexico defends sovereignty as US seeks to label cartels as terrorists
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Tuesday that President Donald Trump's executive order moving toward designating drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations would only impact Mexico if there's close coordination between the two governments.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Suspected settlers attacked Palestinian villages hours before Trump rescinded Biden sanctions
item.description: JINSAFUT, West Bank (AP) — Shortly after suspected Jewish settlers stormed Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank late Monday, setting cars and property ablaze, U.S. President Donald Trump canceled sanctions against Israelis accused of violence in the territory.
item.dc:creator: AREF TUFAHA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WHO defends global health, security role after Trump withdrawal
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) on Tuesday said it "regrets" President Trump's move to withdraw from the global health authority, saying it hopes the U.S. will reconsider the decision.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: World leaders react as Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Leaders across the globe reacted to Donald Trump once again entering the White House, with some nations issuing messages of congratulations while others took issue with his inaugural comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump mistakes Spain for a member of the BRICS bloc and repeats the threat of massive tariffs
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump has apparently confused Spain for a member of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, causing some head-scratching and jitters over possible tariffs in Madrid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli intel indicates Hamas held hostages at new Gaza hospital as UN health agency criticized for inaction
item.description: The IDF recently completed a major raid at the Kamal Adwan Hospital in the northern Gaza Strip, arresting some 240 terrorists. Fox News Digital has learned that hostages were reportedly held there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landslides and flash floods on Indonesia’s Java island leave 17 dead and 8 missing
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian rescuers recovered the bodies of at least 17 people who were swept away in flash floods or buried under tons of mud and rocks that hit hilly villages on the country’s main island of Java, officials said Tuesday. Eight people were missing.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Strong earthquake in Taiwan injures 27 and causes scattered damage
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A 6.4-magnitude earthquake struck southern Taiwan early Tuesday, leaving 27 people with minor injuries and some reported damage. The quake hit at 12:17 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Davos 2025 reacts to Trump tack, EU chief leads defense against climate change
item.description: President Donald Trump has given everyone at Davos something to talk about with his actions on the first day of his second term.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yemen's Houthis signal they'll now limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to Israeli ships
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels signaled Monday they now will limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to only Israeli-affiliated ships after a ceasefire began in the Gaza Strip, but warned wider assaults could resume if needed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Billionaires' wealth soared in 2024, anti-poverty group says as the elites prepare for another Davos
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Billionaires' wealth grew three times faster in 2024 than the year before, a top anti-poverty group reported on Monday as some of the world's political and financial elite prepared for an annual gathering in Davos, Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage speaks out for first time, says she has 'returned to my life' following Hamas captivity
item.description: Emily Damari, one of the first hostages freed by Hamas in the cease-fire agreement, is speaking out for the first time following her release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners as part of cease-fire deal to free hostages
item.description: Israel released 90 Palestinian prisoners to cheering crowds in the West Bank after Hamas returned three hostages that were being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — More than 80 people were killed in the country’s northeast over the weekend following the government's failed attempts to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army, a Colombian official said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the hostages and cease-fire deal between Israel and Hamas set to begin Sunday
item.description: Israel and Hamas finalize a historic cease-fire and hostage deal set to begin Sunday. The agreement includes phased releases, intense negotiations and ongoing security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9
